expectations.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section
expectations.evidence: Full Decomposition Summary with Goal, Likely Affected Areas, Main Workstreams, Highest-Risk Changes, Assumptions, Open Questions, Execution Waves

expectations.text: Tasks are organized into execution waves
expectations.evidence: 7 execution waves with explicit dependency chains and parallelization notes

expectations.text: Every task card includes required fields: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands
expectations.evidence: All 11 cards include the full template: Goal, Context Anchor, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Parallelizable, Can Land Independently, Change Type, Risk Level, Compatibility (API/Data/Rollout), Acceptance Criteria, Verification Prerequisites, Verification Commands, Constraints, Rollout/Rollback Notes, Notes/Risks, Scope Check

expectations.text: Migration is split into separate cards for schema setup, dual-write, backfill, read cutover, and cleanup
expectations.evidence: TASK-03 (schema translation), TASK-04 (DMS replication), TASK-05/06/07 (dual-read/write per service), TASK-09 (read cutover), TASK-10 (write cutover), TASK-11 (cleanup)

expectations.text: High-risk migration cards include rollback notes
expectations.evidence: Feature-flagged routing toggle with 4 states (mysql-only / mysql-primary-pg-shadow / pg-primary-mysql-shadow / pg-only). Each wave notes rollback capability. TASK-10 explicitly notes point of no return.

expectations.text: Accounts for all 3 services needing updates
expectations.evidence: Separate task cards for each service: TASK-05 (Node.js API), TASK-06 (Python/Celery), TASK-07 (Rails admin dashboard) — all in Wave 3, parallelizable

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses zero-downtime with a staged approach
expectations.evidence: DMS continuous replication, dual-read/write phase, staged read cutover (admin first, then Celery, then API), coordinated write cutover with reverse replication safety net

expectations.text: Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section
expectations.evidence: 7 follow-up tasks: performance tuning, PgBouncer, PostgreSQL feature adoption, etc.
